title: Mocking and Test Doubles
content:
In Rust, mocking is done through traits. Define a trait for the dependency, implement it for the real type in production and for a mock type in tests. This is dependency injection via generics or trait objects.

Popular mocking crates like mockall generate mock implementations automatically. But for many cases, simple hand-written mock structs are clearer and more maintainable.

codeExamples.description: Using mockall crate for automatic mocks

keyPoints: Use traits to define dependencies so they can be mocked
Hand-written mocks are simple and explicit for most cases
mockall crate generates mocks automatically with #[automock]
Use RefCell in mocks to record calls through &self methods
Generics (static dispatch) or trait objects (dynamic dispatch) for injection
Test both success and failure paths using mocks
